description: Microarchitecture and ISA security research using pinned design snapshots and controlled experiment slots.

locked_context.data_handling: Keep RTL and experiment artifacts local unless explicitly approved.

slots.prompt: Pin the repository, commit, submodules, RTL configuration, ISA assumptions, target class, tools, reference model, and workloads.
acceptance_criteria: Tools and workloads have stable identifiers.
escalation_conditions: A hardware target or external service requires authorization.

slots.prompt: Map relevant pipeline structures, speculative boundaries, privilege transitions, state, observables, and validation hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Architectural and microarchitectural state are separated.
escalation_conditions: New instrumentation would change the design snapshot.

slots.prompt: Build a threat- and source-bound system model from the research scope, target snapshot, and architecture map.
acceptance_criteria: No hypothesis is presented as fact.
escalation_conditions: A new external source or sensitive artifact is required.

slots.prompt: Define attacker-controlled inputs, protected state, observable signals, privilege, granularity, noise, and confounders.
acceptance_criteria: Control and observability are not assumed.

slots.prompt: Generate distinct, falsifiable security hypotheses linking control, microarchitectural state, protected state, and observation.
acceptance_criteria: Security relevance is distinct from performance variation.
stop_conditions: No hypothesis links control, state, and observation.
escalation_conditions: Testing requires a new target or action class.

slots.prompt: Derive, but do not execute, an authorization-bounded validation matrix that distinguishes hypotheses and countermodels while preserving the pinned snapshot. At A0, use only passive analysis and label active methods as future work behind a new authorization gate.
acceptance_criteria: Every current action is allowed by locked authorization; other methods are future-only or blocked.
stop_conditions: The experiment would change an undeclared controlled variable.
escalation_conditions: A method requires active external testing or unavailable hardware.

slots.prompt: Independently verify the snapshot, architecture mapping, hypotheses, countermodels, experiment matrix, evidence plan, and template challenge.
acceptance_criteria: Functional, microarchitectural, security, and performance claims are separated.
escalation_conditions: Verification discovers unsafe composition, scope drift, or target drift.
